primary_description: Fraction of held-out gold questions each arm answers correctly, graded by the SAME LLM judge (ai/checker.py) into correct-and-useful / wrong / bad-teaching against the gold reference. Identical judge, identical held-out set, both arms. This is the fair primary comparison.
secondary_description: Token-Jaccard >= match_threshold between predicted and gold answer. Reported for transparency; it under-measures correctness for terse gold answers (a correct full-sentence answer rarely shares >= 50% tokens with a two-word gold), so it is NOT the primary metric.
note: AI wins under BOTH metrics; the semantic metric is primary because it measures answer correctness rather than surface token overlap.
